data.current_description:
Query v2: query for any expression.

Unlike `GET /facts`, which only lists facts you've added to Oso Cloud, you can use `POST /evaluate_query` to list derived information about any rule in your policy.
data.summary_suggestion: Evaluate policy expressions and derived rules
data.description_suggestion: Evaluates any expression or rule in your policy and returns derived authorization information. Unlike GET /facts which only returns explicitly added facts, this endpoint can query derived information from policy rules. Requires authentication. Returns results matching the query expression.
data.reasoning: Missing summary entirely. Description mentions comparison to GET /facts but lacks clarity on what is returned, authentication requirements, and the specific use case. Added context about authentication, return values, and clarified the distinction for AI agent understanding.

data.summary_suggestion: Clear all authorization data from Oso Cloud
data.description_suggestion: Removes all facts and authorization data from your Oso Cloud instance. This operation clears all stored facts and resets the authorization state. Requires authentication. Returns success confirmation. This is a destructive operation that cannot be undone.
data.reasoning: No summary or description provided. This is a critical operation with significant side effects. Added comprehensive description explaining the destructive nature, authentication requirement, and return value to help AI agents understand the impact and use this operation appropriately.

data.summary_suggestion: Query available actions for an actor on a resource
data.description_suggestion: Determines which actions an actor can perform on a resource based on your authorization policy. Requires authentication. Accepts an actor, resource, and optional context. Returns a list of permitted actions. This is a local check operation that generates a query for your authorization logic.
data.reasoning: No summary or description provided. Based on the operation name and Local Check API tag, this queries available actions. Added context about inputs (actor, resource, context), outputs (list of actions), and clarified it's a local check operation for AI agent interpretability.

data.current_description: Fetches a query that can be run against your database to determine whether an actor can perform an action on a resource.
data.summary_suggestion: Generate database query for authorization check
data.description_suggestion: Generates a database query that can be executed against your database to determine whether an actor can perform an action on a resource. Requires authentication. Accepts actor, action, and resource parameters. Returns a SQL query or equivalent database query that encodes the authorization logic. This local check operation allows you to integrate authorization checks directly into your database queries.
data.reasoning: Missing summary. Description explains the output but lacks input details and context about authentication. Added summary, clarified input parameters (actor, action, resource), authentication requirement, and emphasized the local check nature for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_description: Fetches a filter that can be applied to a database query to return just the resources on which an actor can perform an action.
data.summary_suggestion: Generate database filter for resource authorization
data.description_suggestion: Generates a database filter that can be applied to a query to return only resources on which an actor can perform a specified action. Requires authentication. Accepts actor, action, and optional context parameters. Returns a filter expression (SQL WHERE clause or equivalent) that encodes the authorization logic. This local check operation enables efficient authorization-aware resource listing.

data.current_description:
Deletes and adds many facts in one atomic transaction. The deletions are performed before the adds. `null` can be used as a wildcard in facts in delete. Does not throw an error when the facts to delete are not found.

DEPRECATED: Prefer `POST /batch` with payload `[{"deletes": <bulk.delete>}, {"inserts": <bulk.tell>}]`.
data.summary_suggestion: Atomically delete and add authorization facts
data.description_suggestion: Deletes and adds authorization facts in a single atomic transaction. Deletions are performed before additions. Supports `null` as a wildcard in delete operations to match multiple facts. Does not raise an error if facts to delete are not found. Note: This endpoint is deprecated; use `POST /batch` instead for new implementations.
data.reasoning: Missing summary entirely. Description includes deprecation notice but lacks clarity about the operation's purpose for AI agents. Added explicit summary and reorganized description to emphasize atomicity, operation order, wildcard support, and error handling behavior. Clarified that this is for authorization facts to align with domain context.

message: Operation 'post_batch' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary. Missing summary. Description is clear and complete, explaining atomicity, in-order processing, wildcard support, and error handling. Only summary suggestion needed.

data.current_description: Deletes and adds many facts in one atomic batch. Facts are inserted and deleted in-order (ie: `insert`ed facts may be `delete`d in the same transaction). `null` can be used as a wildcard in deleted facts. Does not throw an error when the facts to delete are not found.
data.summary_suggestion: Atomically batch delete and insert authorization facts
data.reasoning: Missing summary. Description is clear and complete, explaining atomicity, in-order processing, wildcard support, and error handling. Only summary suggestion needed.

data.current_description:
Query v1: query for any predicate and any combination of concrete and wildcard arguments.

Unlike `GET /facts`, which only lists facts you've added to Oso Cloud, you can use `POST /query` to list derived information about any rule in your policy.
data.summary_suggestion: Query predicates with concrete and wildcard arguments
data.description_suggestion: Queries any predicate in your policy with any combination of concrete values and wildcard arguments. Unlike `GET /facts` which only returns explicitly added facts, this operation returns both facts and derived information computed from policy rules. Supports flexible querying of authorization-relevant data. Note: This is a v1 endpoint; consider newer query operations for specific use cases.

data.current_description: Updates the policy in Oso Cloud. The policy should be represented as a string containing [Polar](https://www.osohq.com/docs/modeling-in-polar/reference) code.
data.summary_suggestion: Update the authorization policy with Polar code
data.description_suggestion: Updates the authorization policy in Oso Cloud with new Polar code. The request body should contain a Policy object with the policy string. Requires API key authentication. Returns 200 on immediate completion or 202 if the policy update is being processed asynchronously. This operation replaces the currently active policy and affects all subsequent authorization decisions.

data.current_description:
Query v2: query for any expression.

Unlike `GET /facts`, which only lists facts you've added to Oso Cloud, you can use `POST /evaluate_query` to list derived information about any rule in your policy.
data.summary_suggestion: Evaluate policy expressions and derived rules
data.description_suggestion: Evaluates any expression or rule in your policy and returns derived authorization information. Unlike GET /facts which only returns explicitly added facts, this endpoint can query derived information from policy rules. Requires authentication. Returns results matching the query expression.
data.reasoning: Missing summary entirely. Description mentions comparison to GET /facts but lacks clarity on what is returned, authentication requirements, and the specific use case. Added context about authentication, return values, and clarified the distinction for AI agent understanding.

This local check operation allows you to integrate authorization checks directly into your database queries.

data.current_description:
Deletes and adds many facts in one atomic transaction. The deletions are performed before the adds. `null` can be used as a wildcard in facts in delete. Does not throw an error when the facts to delete are not found.

DEPRECATED: Prefer `POST /batch` with payload `[{"deletes": <bulk.delete>}, {"inserts": <bulk.tell>}]`.
data.summary_suggestion: Atomically delete and add authorization facts
data.description_suggestion: Deletes and adds authorization facts in a single atomic transaction. Deletions are performed before additions.
